title: Quararibea penduliflora
taxonomy: Quararibea penduliflora belongs to the family Ochnaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a part of the genus Quararibea, which is composed of around 45 species of flowering plants. The plant is often classified as a member of the order Malpighiales, which is a part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Quararibea penduliflora is an evergreen tree with showy flowers and is highly valued for its timber and ornamental purposes. The classification of Quararibea penduliflora is an example of the complex and dynamic nature of plant taxonomy.
uses: Quararibea penduliflora, a plant native to tropical regions, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The bark and roots of the plant are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are used to treat various health conditions. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts are also studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and benefits of Quararibea penduliflora.

constituents: Quararibea penduliflora is known to contain various active constituents, including iridoid glycosides and alkaloids.
